deal_descriptions.short_description: Certiv, a cybersecurity startup focused on securing AI agents on employee workstations, raised $4.2M in a Pre-Seed round from investors including Aviso Ventures, Founders Co-op, and Fortson
deal_descriptions.long_description: Certiv raised $4.2M in a Pre-Seed financing to fund its early commercial and product efforts. The round is described as Pre-Seed capital and lists backers including Aviso Ventures, Founders Co-op, Fortson and other investors. The company said it will use the proceeds to support engineering growth and early enterprise deployments. No prior funding rounds were disclosed in the article. Certiv is working with early customers as it deploys its platform across development, operations and knowledge workflows. The company is led by CEO Jason Needham and is based in Seattle, WA.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Eileen, a next-gen retail technology platform, raised $1M in a pre-seed round led by Top Shelf Ventures.
deal_descriptions.long_description: Eileen Inc. closed a $1 million pre-seed funding round led by Top Shelf Ventures. The deal comprised early-stage equity capital intended to scale the business; no other participating investors or prior rounds were disclosed in the article. The company said the proceeds will be used to scale its AI infrastructure, expand technical talent, and accelerate its go-to-market strategy. Top Shelf Ventures, a VC focused on CPG and related categories, provided a lead investment and public commentary supporting Eileen's approach to retail execution. The press release positioned the funding as a step to broaden Eileen's market presence and product capabilities. The company is based in Pittsburgh.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Datapods, an AI customer-insights startup, raised €500,000 in a Pre-Seed round.
deal_descriptions.long_description: Datapods closed a €500,000 Pre-Seed round to fund development and scaling of its autonomous AI customer-insights platform. The deal is structured as Pre-Seed equity funding totaling €500,000; the articles do not name lead or participating investors. The company says the capital will be used to scale its end-to-end platform and continue building the next generation of AI-powered insights. Datapods was founded and is led by EBS alumnus Finn Rübo together with his team; Rübo completed both EBS Law School (EjP, intake 2019, graduation 2024) and a Business School M.A. in 2024. The raise follows no publicly reported prior funding rounds in the provided articles. The company emphasizes addressing fragmented data by unlocking information behind platforms such as Google, Meta, TikTok and Amazon to provide a holistic view of customer behavior.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Mutable Tactics, a UK defence tech startup, raised $2.1M in Pre-Seed funding led by Seraphim Space with participation from National Security Strategic Investment Fund, Koro, Entrepreneurs First and Transpose
deal_descriptions.long_description: Mutable Tactics completed a $2.1M pre-seed funding round led by Seraphim Space with participation from the National Security Strategic Investment Fund, Koro, Entrepreneurs First and Transpose. The deal is structured as an early-stage pre-seed equity raise; the article does not report any debt or alternative instruments. The company says it will use the proceeds to validate its technology in collaboration with two European governments. Founded in 2024 by Colin MacLeod and Enrique Muñoz de Cote, Mutable Tactics is based in London. No prior funding rounds are mentioned in the article. The raise establishes initial capital to support government validation and further product development.

deal_descriptions.short_description: Buck4Bug, an AI-powered bug bounty platform, raised €430k in a pre-seed round from NGL Ventures' 25 angel syndicate, Plug&Play, and Obeliai Group
deal_descriptions.long_description: Buck4Bug closed a €430k pre-seed round with participation from NGL Ventures' 25 angel syndicate, Plug&Play, and Obeliai Group. The round is a pre-seed financing combining VC/accelerator and angel-syndicate capital. No prior funding rounds are mentioned in the article. In its first 12 months the company built a bug bounty marketplace, assembled 200+ ethical hackers, signed 40+ paying clients, and generated €130k in early revenue. The raise comes as demand for cybersecurity services is growing amid regulatory drivers such as DORA and NIS2. The article does not specify allocation of proceeds.
